expect: Falsification lane rate is 2% vs 20% target. Expect to find: (1) structural cause in open_lane.py or dispatch, (2) >=80% of lanes are exploration/resolution mode, (3) no enforcement mechanism for falsification quota.
actual: 0.13% lifetime rate (1/749 lanes). Three structural causes: (a) open_lane.py two-tier enforcement (L-1225, fixed by S483); (b) dispatch_optimizer.py had zero falsification logic — never suggested WHAT to test; (c) --skip-falsification-check had zero accumulating cost. Mode distribution: objective 28.9%, exploration 22.2%, hardening 12.2%, resolution 11.1%, verification 10.0%, historian 6.7%, assumption 4.4%, replication 3.3%, falsification 1.1%. Built: _print_falsification_advisory() in dispatch_optimizer.py surfaces PHIL claims by weakness+staleness. workspace/falsification-debt.json tracks consecutive skips (3 max before hard-block).
diff: Expected structural cause: CONFIRMED (3 causes, not 1). Expected >=80% non-falsification: CONFIRMED (98.9%). Expected no enforcement: PARTIALLY — enforcement existed but with 3 gaps (tier weakness L-1225, no suggestion, no debt). Novel: agents don't falsify because they face an open-ended search problem, not because they resist mandates.
